heading: Authorities
label: 2799b Authorities
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (e), was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , , which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 90–62982 Stat. 1321section 2751 of this titleOct. 22, 1968

Delegation of FunctionsMemorandum of President of the United States, , 57 F.R. 6663, provided:Feb. 13, 1992Memorandum for the Secretary of State and the Secretary of DefenseBy virtue of the authority vested in me by the Constitution and laws of the United States of America, including of the United States Code, I hereby delegate to the Secretary of Defense the functions vested in me by section 93(a) and section 94 of the Arms Export Control Act, as amended (the “Act”) [, 2799c], and to the Secretary of State the functions vested in me by section 93(f) of the Act. Consistent with section 2 of the Act [], transfers of defense articles under section 93(a) shall be subject to the policy direction of the Secretary of State, including the determination of whether such transfers shall occur.section 301 of title 322 U.S.C. 2799b(a)22 U.S.C. 2752The Secretary of State is authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.George Bush.

children.text: that are battle tanks, armoured combat vehicles, or artillery included within the CFE Treaty’s definition of “conventional armaments and equipment limited by the Treaty”;
that were, as of the date of signature of the CFE Treaty, in the stocks of the Department of Defense and located in the CFE Treaty’s area of application; and
that the President determines are not needed by United States military forces within the CFE Treaty’s area of application.

children.heading: Acceptance of NATO assistance in eliminating direct costs of transfers
children.label: b Acceptance of NATO assistance in eliminating direct costs of transfers
children.text: In order to eliminate direct costs of facilitating transfers of defense articles under subsection (a), the United States may utilize services provided by NATO or any NATO/CFE country, including inspection, repair, or transportation services with respect to defense articles so transferred.

children.heading: Acceptance of NATO assistance in meeting certain United States obligations
children.label: c Acceptance of NATO assistance in meeting certain United States obligations
children.text: In order to facilitate United States compliance with the CFE Treaty-mandated obligations for destruction of conventional armaments and equipment limited by the CFE Treaty, the United States may utilize services or funds provided by NATO or any NATO/CFE country.

children.heading: Authority to transfer on grant basis
children.label: d Authority to transfer on grant basis
children.text: Defense articles may be transferred under subsection (a) without cost to the recipient country.

children.heading: Third country transfers restrictions
children.label: e Third country transfers restrictions
children.text: For purposes of sections 2753(a)(2), 2753(a)(3), 2753(c), and 2753(d) of this title, defense articles transferred under subsection (a) shall be deemed to have been sold under this chapter.

children.heading: Maintenance of military balance in Eastern Mediterranean
children.label: f Maintenance of military balance in Eastern Mediterranean
children.text: The President shall ensure that transfers by the United States under subsection (a), taken together with transfers by other NATO/CFE countries in implementing the CFE Treaty, are of such valuations so as to be consistent with the United States policy, embodied in , of maintaining the military balance in the Eastern Mediterranean.section 2373 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the authority of subsection (a) expires at the end of the 40-month period beginning on the date on which the CFE Treaty enters into force.

children.heading: Transition rule
children.label: 2 Transition rule
children.text: Paragraph (1) does not apply with respect to a transfer of defense articles for which notification under is submitted before the end of the period described in that paragraph.section 2799c(a) of this title
